title: Top 10 tech stocks showing highest dividend yield amid rising volatility
content:
[Man looking at currency trading app on his smart phone from his home office]
Alistair Berg

Amid broader markets grappling with geopolitical uncertainty, oil prices above $115 per barrel, and renewed concerns over global logistics disruptions due to Strait of Hormuz tensions, investors are likely watching out for pockets of stability.

Here, we are scanning the technology sector for reliable dividend payers. Below is a list of tech companies currently offering some of the highest dividend yields in the sector.

 	* Autoscope Technologies (AATC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AATC]) - Dividend Yield - 10.40% 
 	* Taitron Components (TAIT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TAIT]) - Dividend Yield - 9.27% 
 	* Network-1 Technologies (NTIP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NTIP]) - Dividend Yield - 6.76% 
 	* HP (HPQ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HPQ]) - Dividend Yield - 6.17%
 	* NVE Corporation (NVEC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVEC]) - Dividend Yield - 5.97%
 	* Xerox (XRX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XRX]) - Dividend Yield - 5.75%
 	* George Risk Industries (RSKIA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RSKIA]) - Dividend Yield - 5.62%
 	* Skyworks Solutions (SWKS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SWKS]) - Dividend Yield - 5.18%
 	* MCAP (MCAP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MCAP]) - Dividend Yield - 4.94%
 	* Immersion (IMMR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IMMR]) - Dividend Yield - 4.93%
